header_summary:
The discussions across various mailing lists highlight significant advancements and theoretical explorations in the field of cryptography as it pertains to blockchain technology. A notable focus is on the computational and financial aspects of finding a hash collision between scripts in Bitcoin, a process detailed by the ColliderScript project. This endeavor, requiring an estimated 2^81 operations and potentially costing between $100,000 to $2 million per collision, underscores the technical challenges and the innovative approaches being considered to address them, with further insights available in the [ColliderScript paper](https://eprint.iacr.org/2024/1802.pdf).

Another area of exploration is the proposed Ladder Script, aimed at enhancing Bitcoin's transaction scripting capabilities. This proposal seeks to introduce structured spending conditions, improve script extensibility, and incorporate post-quantum signatures without the need for new opcodes, potentially through a soft fork, as detailed in resources provided at [https://bitcoinghost.org/labs/](https://bitcoinghost.org/labs/) and [https://github.com/defenwycke/ghost-labs-ladder-script](https://github.com/defenwycke/ghost-labs-ladder-script). 

The discussion also delves into the complexities of isogeny-based cryptography (IBC), emphasizing its potential for secure cryptographic protocols and addressing the challenges of integrating desirable cryptographic properties such as rerandomization and batch verification into blockchain technologies. A pioneering effort in this direction is demonstrated by [SQIsignHD](https://eprint.iacr.org/2023/436), which explores signature compression at the cost of verifier performance.

Lastly, the release of UltrafastSecp256k1 version 3.3 marks a significant advancement in the performance and security of the secp256k1 library, with improvements ranging from batch operation speeds to Schnorr batch verification optimizations. This update, described as the fastest and most secure implementation to date, is detailed further for developers and users at [https://github.com/shrec/UltrafastSecp256k1/releases/tag/v3.3](https://github.com/shrec/UltrafastSecp256k1/releases/tag/v3.3), showcasing the ongoing commitment to enhancing cryptocurrency infrastructure through open-source collaboration.

recent_posts.title: [BIP proposal] Pay to Schnorr Key Hash (P2SKH)
authors: wrapped Taproot" from RIPEMD-160 collisions, Was:
recent_posts.summary:
- Finding a hash collision requires about 2^81 operations and immense storage, highlighting its impracticality.
- Potential solutions include time-memory tradeoffs, significantly reducing resource needs with ColliderScript insights.
- Using ASICs for this task may cost between $100,000 to $2 million per collision, offering a cheaper alternative.

recent_posts.title: [Draft BIP proposal] Ladder script
recent_posts.summary:
- Ladder Script proposes transaction version 4, enhancing Bitcoin Script with structured conditions.
- It features post-quantum signatures and anti-spam measures, aiming for transaction security.
- A soft fork integrates Ladder Script into Bitcoin, supported by a draft BIP and C++ development.

recent_posts.title: Compact Isogeny PQC can replace HD wallets, key-tweaking, silent payments
recent_posts.summary:
- Isogeny-based cryptography hinges on selecting specific isogenies as secret keys for security.
- It diverges from classical ECC by complicating isogeny composition, despite facilitating group homomorphisms.
- Current research, like SQIsignHD, explores enhancing IBC through methods like signature compression.

recent_posts.title: UltrafastSecp256k1 v3.3
recent_posts.summary:
- UltrafastSecp256k1 v3.3 significantly enhances secp256k1 library performance and security on various backends.
- It improves batch operations speed by 17 to 67 times, introducing an all-affine accumulation strategy.
- The update also boosts OpenCL speed by 10% and optimizes Schnorr batch verification for systems like Lightning Network.

recent_posts.title: Binohash: Transaction Introspection Without Softforks
recent_posts.summary:
- Binohash employs a novel blockchain method, avoiding direct tx data access, using sighash mutation.
- This pseudo-introspection method signifies a shift in handling blockchain transactions securely.
- It suggests potential for innovative blockchain solutions beyond traditional data introspection limits.

active_posts.title: [BIP proposal] Pay to Schnorr Key Hash (P2SKH)
active_posts.summary:
- Saint Wenhao proposes a new Bitcoin output type P2SKH for transaction efficiency.
- P2SKH combines P2WPKH's compactness with P2TR's Schnorr signatures, enhancing privacy.
- Concerns include computational overhead and potential BIP360 conflicts, with community feedback sought.

active_posts.title: [Draft BIP proposal] Ladder script
active_posts.summary:
- Ladder Script proposes a new Bitcoin transaction version to improve script extensibility and analysis.
- It introduces structured spending conditions, aiming for enhanced security and reduced transaction ambiguity.
- The proposal includes a development and testing framework, inviting community feedback for refinement.

active_posts.title: Great Consensus Cleanup Revival
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal examines Bitcoin protocol flaws, suggesting improvements for security and efficiency.
- It proposes adjustments to mining and transaction rules to tackle vulnerabilities and inefficiencies.
- Community debate arises over suggestions like reducing block size, highlighting scalability concerns.

active_posts.title: CTV+CSFS: Can we reach consensus on a first step towards covenants?
active_posts.summary:
- Covenants in Bitcoin allow conditions for spending, enhancing security and smart contracts.
- These mechanisms enable innovative applications like trustless escrow and safer DeFi solutions.
- A dedicated wiki page offers extensive details on Bitcoin's covenant support and applications.

active_posts.title: A simple backup scheme for wallet accounts
active_posts.summary:
- Josh's method has unique inscription and location aspects.
- Suggests standardizing Salvatore's method, then adding Josh's features.
- Aims to merge both methods for a comprehensive solution.

today_in_history_posts.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The April 6, 2021 meeting focused on implementing technical proposals quickly.
- Debate existed over using heights or MTP for changes, with no firm consensus.
- A November 15 activation date was aimed for, despite differing views on UASF timing.

today_in_history_posts.title: Increase channel-jamming capital requirements by not counting dust HTLCs
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Eugene proposes increasing capital requirements to mitigate channel-jamming attacks.
- He suggests trimming dust HTLC outputs to prevent low-capital attackers from jamming channels.
- This measure aims to ensure commitment transactions remain valid without affecting non-dust throughput.

today_in_history_posts.title: Improving transaction sponsor blockspace efficiency
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Jeremy Rubin's revised proposal embeds the txid within a signature to enhance transaction sponsorship.
- The new method allows sponsoring of multiple transactions, significantly reducing block space overhead.
- It introduces a market for transaction sponsorship, optimizing blockchain efficiency and collaboration.
